UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a citizen's basic health profile and authentication linkage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

name.description: The full name of the user.

gender.description: The user's self-declared gender (e.g., 'Male', 'Female', 'Other', 'Prefer not to say').

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for application interface and communications (e.g., 'en', 'hi').

city.description: The user's current city of residence.

emergencyContactName.description: The name of the user's emergency contact person.

emergencyContactPhone.description: The phone number of the user's emergency contact.

abhaId.description: The user's Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) ID, if available. This field is optional.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

TriageAssessment.title: TriageAssessment
TriageAssessment.description: Records a single AI-powered symptom triage assessment performed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TriageAssessment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this assessment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N TriageAssessment)

symptomsInput.description: The raw text input of symptoms provided by the user (either typed or speech-to-text transcription).

assessmentResult.description: The AI's suggested severity and next steps (e.g., 'home care', 'clinic visit', 'emergency', 'hospital').

suggestedNextSteps.description: Detailed natural language recommendations provided by the AI based on the assessment result.

assessmentDate.description: Timestamp when the triage assessment was performed.

rawAiResponse.description: Optional raw JSON or text response from the AI model for auditing or detailed analysis.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The `UserProfile.id` field matches the Firebase Authentication UID (`userId` from the path). Access is restricted to the user whose `uid` matches `userId` in the path, ensuring strong ownership-based security. No denormalized authorization fields are required as ownership is path-based and implicit for single user profile access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores a chronological record of AI triage assessments performed by a specific user. Each `TriageAssessment` document includes the `userId` field, which is explicitly denormalized from the parent user. This ensures authorization independence by allowing rules to verify ownership without requiring a `get()` call to the parent document. Access is restricted to the user whose `uid` matches `userId` in the path, supporting secure listing of a user's own assessment history.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user who performed the assessment, linking it to the parent UserProfile.

params.name: triageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific triage assessment.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):**
    *   **Path:** `/users/{userId}`
    *   **Entity:** `UserProfile`
    *   **Authorization Independence:** The `UserProfile` document itself is identified by the Firebase Authentication UID (`userId` parameter). The document's `id` field will explicitly store this `uid`. This makes authorization inherently independent as `request.auth.uid` must match `userId` for read/write access. There are no `get()` calls to parent documents needed to determine access.
    *   **Structural Segregation:** Each user's profile is a private document, accessible only by the owning user. This maintains a homogeneous security posture for all documents within the `/users` collection, where each document represents a unique, private user profile.
    *   **QAPs (Queries as Authorization Predicates):** A user can securely read or update their own profile by specifying `users/$(request.auth.uid)`. Listing of all user profiles would be inherently disallowed or strictly managed by an admin role (not defined in this scope), as it requires matching `request.auth.uid` to the specific `userId` in the path, preventing unauthorized `list` operations from revealing other users' data.

2.  **Triage Assessments (`/users/{userId}/triageAssessments/{triageId}`):**
    *   **Path:** `/users/{userId}/triageAssessments/{triageId}`
    *   **Entity:** `TriageAssessment`
    *   **Authorization Independence:** Each `TriageAssessment` document is stored as a subcollection under the specific `UserProfile` that created it. Crucially, the `TriageAssessment` document includes a `userId` field that explicitly stores the ID of the owning user. This denormalization means that even though it's a subcollection, its authorization does not depend on reading the parent `UserProfile` document. Rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path) and also optionally verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` (from the document itself) without a `get()` call.
    *   **Structural Segregation:** All documents within a user's `/triageAssessments` subcollection are private to that user. This creates a clear boundary and simplifies security rules, as all documents in this subcollection share the same access requirements.
    *   **QAPs (Queries as Authorization Predicates):** A user can securely `list` all their past triage assessments by querying `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/triageAssessments`. The security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` (the parent parameter), ensuring that a user can only list *their own* assessments. This structure prevents unauthorized users from performing wide `list` queries across all triage assessments and effectively supports QAPs for retrieving personal history.
